text: Git branching is a core concept in version control that helps teams work on code simultaneously. Typically, developers create feature branches from the main codebase to work on new features or bug fixes without affecting the stable version. Once the feature is complete and tested, they merge it back into the main branch. In many workflows, this is done through pull requests, where team members can review the code, suggest changes, and discuss improvements before the merge is finalized. This structured process ensures a clean version control workflow and reduces the risk of introducing errors into the project.
